name: ca-certificates
title: ca-certificates
description:
Common CA certificates
Contains the certificate authorities shipped with Mozilla's browser to allow
SSL-based applications to check for the authenticity of SSL connections.
.
Please note that Debian can neither confirm nor deny whether the
certificate authorities whose certificates are included in this package
have in any way been audited for trustworthiness or RFC 3647 compliance.
Full responsibility to assess them belongs to the local system
administrator.

arguments.description: update /etc/ssl/certs and ca-certificates.crt
Change the configuration file. By default, the file /etc/ca-certificates.conf is used.
arguments.raw: --certsconf  Change the configuration file. By default, the file /etc/ca-certificates.conf is used.

arguments.description: Change the certificate directory. By default, the directory /usr/share/ca-certificates
arguments.raw: --certsdir  Change the certificate directory. By default, the directory /usr/share/ca-certificates

arguments.description: Change the /etc certificate directory. By default, the directory /etc/ssl/certs is
arguments.raw: --etccertsdir  Change the /etc certificate directory. By default, the directory /etc/ssl/certs is

arguments.description: Fresh updates. Remove symlinks in /etc/ssl/certs directory.
arguments.raw: -f  Fresh updates. Remove symlinks in /etc/ssl/certs directory.

arguments.description: Fresh updates. Remove symlinks in /etc/ssl/certs directory.
arguments.raw: --fresh  Fresh updates. Remove symlinks in /etc/ssl/certs directory.

arguments.description: Show summary of options.
Show summary of options.
the local certificate directory. By default, the directory /usr/local/share/ca-
arguments.raw: --localcertsdir  the local certificate directory. By default, the directory /usr/local/share/ca-

arguments.description: Be verbose. Output openssl rehash.
Be verbose. Output openssl rehash.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use update-ca-certificates because Common CA certificates.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use update-ca-certificates as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `update-ca-certificates [options]` as documented upstream for update-ca-certificates.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair update-ca-certificates with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `update-ca-certificates --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
